wine_museums.name: Musee des Vins d'Alsace
wine_museums.address: 12 Place de la Cathedrale, 68000 Colmar, France
wine_museums.description: Cathedral-square museum immersing visitors in Alsace terroir and history, with an audio-guided trail in seven languages ending in a three-glass tasting of Riesling, Gewurztraminer and Pinot Noir.
wine_museums.admission: Adult EUR 15, reduced EUR 13, children 3-11 EUR 9, under-3 free

wine_museums.name: Musee du Vignoble et des Vins d'Alsace
wine_museums.description: Inside the Chateau de la Confrerie Saint-Etienne, the heritage museum of Alsace viticulture with antique presses, foudres and 18th-century cellar tools across two thematic floors.
wine_museums.admission: Adult EUR 5, reduced EUR 3, children under 16 free

wine_museums.name: Cave Historique des Hospices Civils de Strasbourg
wine_museums.description: 1,200 sqm 14th-century medieval cellar inside Strasbourg's Civil Hospital, home to a barrel-aged white wine from 1472 and a working cellar producing Alsace wines.
wine_museums.admission: Free entry to cellar; private tasting visits by reservation

verified.address_quoted: Cave Historique - 1 place de l'hopital 67091 STRASBOURG

wine_museums.name: Ecomusee d'Alsace
wine_museums.description: 97-hectare open-air museum with more than 80 reconstructed Alsace buildings, including a dedicated wine quarter with old presses, foudres and the vintner workshop.
wine_museums.admission: Adult EUR 18, reduced EUR 13, family pass EUR 53

wine_museums.name: Musee des Eaux de Vie Lapoutroie
wine_museums.address: 85 Rue du General Dufieux, 68650 Lapoutroie, France
wine_museums.description: Eau-de-vie museum inside an 1832 post-station building in Lapoutroie, run by Distillerie Paul Devoille. 30th most-visited site in Alsace with copper stills, antique presses and tasting bar.
wine_museums.admission: Adult EUR 6, reduced EUR 4, children under 12 free

wine_museums.name: Bibliotheque Humaniste de Selestat
wine_museums.address: 1 Place Docteur Maurice Kubler, 67600 Selestat, France
wine_museums.description: UNESCO Memory of the World Register library in Selestat, housing 15th and 16th-century manuscripts on Alsace viticulture and Beatus Rhenanus' humanist library; Ricciotti-designed museum building.
wine_museums.admission: Adult EUR 7, reduced EUR 5, children under 18 free
